_meta.description: Evidence contract — facts-only field definitions for LoRA evaluation. Three object types: Method, Eval, Sample.
notes: Evidence is nested: related fields live under domain-object parents (e.g. lora.hash, conditioning.positive_hash).
Fields may hold a normal value OR an Invalid wrapper {status: NULL|ERROR|NOT_APPLICABLE, ...}.
invalid_wrapper: true means the field may legally hold an Invalid wrapper instead of a normal value.
required: true means Bouncer emits SCHEMA.MISSING_FIELD (WARN/ERROR) if the field is absent.
vital: true means Bouncer hard-refuses the entire record if the field is absent (nothing written to DB).
Unknown keys at ingestion time are separated into _extras — never stored in the core record.
GENERATIVE vs MEASUREMENT SPLIT: Method record contains only generative harness inputs (fields that
causally determine the output, minus LoRA/seed/strength). Measurement instruments (ClipVision,
InsightFace, masks, aux images) are NOT hash-components and are stored in sample_record measurement domains.
See Constitution: Generative inputs vs measurement instruments.
OPEN-ENDED KEYSPACES: clip_vision keyed by model_hash[:16] (per S-21); masks and aux keyed by
user-provided name strings. Only these three keyspaces are open-ended; all other domains are fixed-key.
SAMPLE IDENTITY: sample.hash = BLAKE3(eval.hash, seed, lora_strength). Deterministic before generation.
latent_hash is stored as a vital integrity field for NOT_ZERO_DELTA detection, NOT as the identity key.
REVIEW OUTPUTS: derived comparison metrics like img_px_diff and clip_global_cos_dist are assembled review outputs,
NOT Evidence fields. Evidence stores raw facts only.

method_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of canonical JSON over these fields (null hashes as JSON null, not as Invalid wrapper)

eval_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of (method_hash, lora.hash). lora.hash is null for Baseline evals.

sample_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of (eval_hash, seed, lora_strength). lora_strength is null for Baseline samples.

invalid_wrapper.description: Wraps a missing, failed, or inapplicable field value. Never use JSON null.

valueref.description: Portable content-addressed reference to a non-JSON asset (embedding, image, mask, etc.).

asset_type.description: Semantic type: embedding | image | mask | luminance | aux

path.description: Relative path to the asset under assets_root

dtype.description: Data type for array/tensor assets (e.g. float32)

pixel_stats.description: Basic pixel statistics inline in Evidence (avoids loading asset for simple queries).

diagnostic_record.description: A structured diagnostic record attached to a sample.

code.description: Stable dot-separated identifier from diagnostic_codes registry

method_record.description: Generative harness: everything that causally determines an output except LoRA identity, seed, and LoRA strength. Stored in Methods table.

method_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of all Method HashComponents (canonical serialization). Primary key and dedup key.

extractor_version.description: Semantic version of the extractor that produced this record

is_dirty.description: True if any error condition detected on this Method; taints all Evals/Samples under it

base_model.description: Base model checkpoint used for sampling

hash.description: BLAKE3 fast-hash of base model file (header + first 64KB of tensor data) — HashComponent

vae_model.description: VAE used to decode the latent. Part of method.hash because different VAE = different decoded image.

hash.description: BLAKE3 fast-hash of VAE file — HashComponent

conditioning.description: Conditioning tensors used during sampling. Hashed by content, not by prompt text.

positive_hash.description: BLAKE3 of the positive conditioning tensor(s) — HashComponent

positive_text.description: Raw positive prompt text — informational only, not a HashComponent

positive_guidance.description: Per-conditioning guidance scale if present (Flux/SDXL embedded guidance) — HashComponent; null hashes as-is

negative_hash.description: BLAKE3 of the negative conditioning tensor(s) — HashComponent; required even if all-zero (Flux)

negative_text.description: Raw negative prompt text — informational only, not a HashComponent

negative_guidance.description: Per-conditioning guidance scale for negative (if present) — HashComponent; null hashes as-is

settings.description: KSampler settings that causally affect the output — all are HashComponents

latent.description: Input latent dimensions — HashComponents; null values hash as-is

shape.description: Full latent tensor shape [B, C, H_latent, W_latent]. C=4 for SD/SDXL, C=16 for SD3/Flux.

model_extras.description: Fingerprint of any non-base, non-LoRA pipeline extras attached via model_options (e.g. custom CFG wrappers, attention patches) — HashComponent; null for standard configurations

workflow_ref.description: Stored workflow declaration artifact used to orchestrate or replay this Method. Informational/orchestration-only; not a HashComponent and not Method identity.

eval_record.description: LoRA identity under a Method. Groups all (seed × lora_strength) samples for one (method + LoRA) pair. Stored in Evals table.

eval_hash.description: BLAKE3 of (method_hash, lora.hash). Primary key and dedup key.

method_hash.description: Foreign key to parent Method record

is_dirty.description: True if any error condition detected on this Eval

lora.description: LoRA adapter under evaluation. For Baseline evals, all fields are null/Invalid — lora.hash null hashes as-is in eval_hash computation.

hash.description: BLAKE3 canonical content hash of LoRA weight tensors — primary LoRA identity; null for Baseline

file_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of raw LoRA .safetensors file bytes — audit/forensics; null for Baseline

name.description: Display LoRA name — informational only, not a HashComponent

rank.description: Inferred LoRA rank (network dimension) from safetensors tensor shapes

network_alpha.description: LoRA network alpha scaling value extracted from safetensors metadata

target_blocks.description: Unique targeted block prefixes inferred from tensor keys

affects_text_encoder.description: True when LoRA tensor keys indicate text encoder influence

raw_metadata.description: Raw __metadata__ dictionary extracted from LoRA safetensors header

sample_record.description: Atomic generated output: one (eval + seed + lora_strength) replicate. Stores measurement domains as ValueRefs + inline stats. Stored in Samples table.

sample_hash.description: BLAKE3 of (eval_hash, seed, lora_strength). Primary key and dedup key. Deterministic before generation.

eval_hash.description: Foreign key to parent Eval record

seed.description: RNG seed used for this sample — HashComponent

lora_strength.description: LoRA conditioning strength — HashComponent; null for Baseline samples (hashes as-is)

latent_hash.description: BLAKE3 of the output latent tensor bytes. NOT the identity key. Used for NOT_ZERO_DELTA detection: if same sample_hash arrives with different latent_hash → both samples dirty.

image_hash.description: BLAKE3 of the decoded image tensor bytes. Used for slot consistency: if same sample_hash arrives with different image_hash → SAMPLE.SLOT_CONFLICT.

evidence_version.description: Evidence contract version active when this record was written (e.g. 2.0.0)

ingest_status.description: OK if clean; WARN for non-fatal anomalies; ERROR if any field had status=ERROR

is_dirty.description: True if Bouncer detected any structural error; review assembly excludes dirty samples by default

fields._note_image: image.output is a ValueRef to the decoded output image asset (.npy or .png). Pixel stats stored inline.

output.description: ValueRef to decoded output image asset

shape.description: Actual decoded image shape [H, W, C] — inline for quick dimension queries and to validate that actual decoded dimensions match expected latent.width × latent.height

fields._note_luminance: BT.709 per-pixel luminance map Y = 0.2126R + 0.7152G + 0.0722B. Computed internally at extraction time (never from external preprocessor). Coefficients must be consistent across all samples.
fields._note_clip_vision: OPEN-ENDED KEYSPACE. Keys are model_hash[:16] (first 16 hex chars of CLIP Vision model parameter hash). Keyed by model identity, not slot index, to avoid the slot-index trap (S-21). Per slot: global_embedding (full-image encode), last_hidden_state, shape metadata, is_aliased flag, stats dict, patch_pools dict. Masked ClipVision approaches (S-31) not yet implemented — reserved for future phase.

clip_vision.description: Per-model ClipVision outputs. Each key is the first 16 hex chars of the CV model parameter hash.

last_hidden_state.description: Full last hidden state from CLIP Vision encoder (.npy)

model_hash.description: Full CLIP Vision model parameter hash (inline; slot key is model_hash[:16])

global_embedding_shape.description: Shape of the global embedding array

last_hidden_state_shape.description: Shape of the last hidden state array

is_aliased.description: True if this slot is an alias (same model reused under a different name)

stats.description: Inline statistics for the global embedding (e.g. norm, mean)

patch_pools.description: Patch-pooled embeddings dict (future masked ClipVision approach)

fields._note_masks: OPEN-ENDED KEYSPACE. Default set: face, background, skin, clothing, hair. Custom names accepted. All masks are provided externally by the user (segmentation preprocessors). Stored as .npy ValueRefs. Inline stats stored for quick queries without loading asset.

masks.description: Per-mask binary arrays and stats. Keys are mask names.

edge_density.description: Sobel edge magnitude sum within masked region, normalized

fields._note_face_analysis: InsightFace ArcFace 512-dim identity embedding. Singleton per sample (best face by det_score). Raw embedding + L2-normalized embedding stored as ValueRefs (.npy); hash + stats + pose stored inline.

embedding.description: ValueRef to 512-dim float32 ArcFace identity vector (.npy)

embedding_hash.description: BLAKE3 of the ArcFace vector — for quick identity comparison without loading asset

embedding_norm.description: L2 norm of the ArcFace vector

face_count.description: Number of faces detected by InsightFace

det_score.description: Confidence score of the best detected face

bbox.description: Bounding box of the best face: [x0, y0, x1, y1]

normed_embedding.description: L2-normalized ArcFace identity embedding (.npy float32 [512]); used for cosine similarity comparisons by compare.face.primary_identity_cos.v1

pose.description: InsightFace head pose estimate for the best detected face

fields._note_aux: OPEN-ENDED KEYSPACE. Default live set: depth, normal, edge. Custom preprocessor labels accepted (S-34). Aux images are provided externally by the user — the Extractor does NOT run preprocessors internally. The user pairs each aux image with a STRING label; the label becomes the dict key and documents which preprocessor was used. Legacy raster aux.pose is retired; pose analysis now lives in pose_evidence.

aux.description: Auxiliary preprocessor images. Keys are user-provided labels (for example depth, normal, edge). Legacy raster pose maps are retired; pose analysis now lives in pose_evidence.

pixel_stats.description: Inline pixel statistics for the aux image

fields._note_pose_evidence: Fixed-key structured pose evidence. Stores both the raw grouped upstream people payload and the normalized interpreted per-person evidence with support facts against MainSubject and DensePose. This is the live pose-analysis surface; legacy raster aux.pose is retired.

pose_evidence.description: Structured grouped pose evidence keyed by supported body-pose source.

diagnostics.description: Structured diagnostic records emitted during extraction and validation for this sample
